steps.title: Locate the vertical intercept
steps.content: A $y$-intercept is where the graph crosses the $y$-axis. Every point on the $y$-axis has $x=0$.

steps.title: Read the coordinate
steps.content: From the graph, the curve crosses the $y$-axis at $(0,3)$.

steps.title: Give the vertical coordinate
steps.content: The question asks for the $y$-coordinate of the intercept, which is the second coordinate of $(0,3)$. The answer is 3.
